message:
fix(reactor/io_uring): add modify() rollback semantics and stale-registration pruning

The io_uring modify() method previously updated bookkeeping before
confirming the kernel-side poll_add succeeded. If the SQE submission
failed, the registration's interest field was left in an inconsistent
state — recording an interest the kernel never saw.

Refactor modify() to:
1. Read the old interest before making any changes
2. Submit poll_remove + poll_add for the new interest
3. Only update bookkeeping on success
4. On failure: attempt to re-submit the old poll_add (best-effort revert
   to prior state)
5. On EBADF/ENOENT: prune the stale registration from bookkeeping
   entirely, since the fd is no longer valid

This mirrors the epoll backend's approach where deregister is best-effort
and closed fds are cleaned up rather than left as stale entries.

New test: test_modify_closed_fd_prunes_stale_registration creates a
Unix stream pair, registers it, closes the fd, attempts modify, and
verifies the registration is pruned to count 0 with a subsequent
deregister returning NotFound.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
